entry.title: SAP BTP Hackathon at SAPHILA 2025 (AFSUG Members)
entry.content:
This year we launched the first-ever AFSUG and SAP collaboration SAP BTP Hackathon.

In a nutshell:

  • All AFSUG members can nominate a business use case that showcases SAP BTP capabilities
  • Up to 25 customers will be selected to build out their use case and compete for a chance to demo it live on stage at SAPHILA 2025
  • 8 finalists will participate in the first-ever SAPHILA Demo Jam in June for a chance to win some cool prizes

When talking to Amanda Gibbs, the CEO of the African SAP User Group (AFSUG), she shared her thoughts:

"The AFSUG SAP BTP Hackathon is an extraordinary opportunity for our members to showcase their creativity, problem-solving skills, and innovative thinking. By collaborating with SAP on the SAPHILA Hackathon, we’re creating a platform where customers and partners can explore the transformative potential of SAP BTP, turning bold ideas into tangible solutions that deliver real business value. At AFSUG, we believe in the power of community and shared learning. This hackathon is not just about competition; it’s about fostering connections, gaining insights, and driving meaningful change together. I encourage all our members to seize this chance to innovate, learn from SAP’s global experts, and be part of something truly impactful."

I really can’t beat that intro, so I am not even going to try 🙂

 

The Details

SAP BTP is THE platform that brings together application development, automation, data management, analytics and planning, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for integrating and extending SAP applications.

It’s used by customers, partners and SAP to innovate and optimize business processes while leveraging the latest and greatest cloud technology.

The SAP BTP hackathon challenge will showcase how customers can use SAP BTP to adapt and optimize their business processes. But that’s not all!

AFSUG members will also benefit from in-depth enablement sessions led by SAP product experts to help them through the ideation and build phases.

The participating customers, those who have had their use case selected for the SAP BTP hackathon, will also have access to expert coaches during their build phase. Coaches are there to make sure “no use case is left behind” 😉

Once the build phase is completed, customers will pitch their use case to a jury of SAP experts. The jury will choose 8 finalists based on different criteria, like business relevance, SAP BTP usage, innovation and vision, that will go on to demo their use case in front of a live audience at SAPHILA 2025.

To show off your efforts, participating customers will get special badges added to their SAP Community profile. So, if you are not yet an SAP Community member, what are you waiting for?

 

To All AFSUG Members

Don’t miss this unique opportunity to participate in the SAP BTP hackathon!

In short:

  1. Submit your use case here by February 18
  2. (Selected customers) Build your prototype by April 22
  3. Get ready to pitch your demo from May 2 to May 16
  4. (Finalists) Compete in the Demo Jam at SAPHILA 2025 on June 3

In case you still need convincing, let me reiterate some of the perks:

  • Attend SAP BTP enablement sessions led by SAP experts for inspiration and knowledge.
  • Engage with the SAP ecosystem and get special badges to display on your profile. Don’t forget to create an account in the SAP Community
  • Get direct access to SAP BTP coaches to support you during your build phase.
  • Expand your SAP BTP knowledge and bridge the gap between business and IT.

 

Conclusion

We are beyond excited to see what our customers come up and see their ideas come to life with SAP Business Technology Platform.

And yes, we would love to bring this format to other user groups and locations. One step at a time 😉

entry.title: Implico at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
As the energy sector navigates rapid digital transformation and sustainability challenges, Implico is at the forefront of delivering innovative solutions to optimize supply chains and enhance fuel retail operations. From April 8-10, 2025, in Rotterdam, we will join leading energy and technology experts at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference and showcase how cutting-edge automation and SAP solutions can empower companies to adapt and thrive in a changing market. 

We are proud to present two key sessions at this event: a workshop session on the Pre-Conference Day April 8 and a co-customer presentation at the main event on April 9, both featuring real-world applications of our solutions.

 

1. Optimizing Fuel Retail Operations with SAP Solutions 

Speakers: Jean-Marc Delbos (SAP) & Thomas Weggelaar (Implico) 

Efficient management of fuel retail networks is essential for profitability and customer satisfaction. This joint session with Jean-Marc Delbos from SAP and Thomas Weggelaar from Implico will focus on enhancing the end-to-end processes of fuel retail operations using SAP’s Retail Fuel Network Operations (SAP S/4HANA RFNO) solution and Implico’s automation technologies. 

 Key Takeaways: 

  • Unlocking operational efficiencies through SAP S/4HANA RFNO and automated data flows. 
  • Real-time fuel inventory monitoring and replenishment. 
  • Reducing operational overhead and carbon emissions through process automation. 

 

2. Automatic Trip Planning for Bulk Logistics 

Speaker: Adrian Mägerle (Migrol/Switzerland) & André Bente (Implico) 

(IDM) by Implico is a robust solution designed to enhance capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. Fuel distribution logistics is a complex puzzle that requires precision and efficiency. In this session, Adrian Mägerle from Migrol AG, one of Switzerland’s leading energy services providers, will explore together with André Bente from Implico how automatic trip planning optimizes the logistics in supply chain and secondary distribution by leveraging SAP-integrated tools and Implico’s Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) capabilities. 

Key Takeaways: 

  • Reducing manual planning efforts  
  • Boosting efficiency and sustainability 
  • Insights from a prototype of the new Implico SAPlement AddTCO 

 

Enhancing Sustainability through Digitalization and the Clean Core Approach 

As an SAP SOLEX Partner, Implico’s approach to sustainability focuses on combining digitalization, automation, and optimized resource allocation with SAP’s Clean Core approach, which ensures that custom business processes are decoupled from the digital core of SAP systems. This strategy allows for easier upgrades, continuous innovation, and long-term system stability. 

By leveraging clean, structured data and the Clean Core methodology, Implico enables: 

  • Efficient transport routes that reduce fuel consumption and emissions. 
  • Real-time energy usage monitoring and sustainability tracking. 
  • Smarter asset utilization to minimize waste and maximize operational efficiency. 

These solutions are designed to meet both current needs and the challenges of a greener future, including the integration of LNG, hydrogen, and other renewable fuels into existing networks. 

 

Join Us in Rotterdam for Insightful Discussions and Live Demos 

We invite attendees to visit our booth for hands-on demos of our solutions and to attend our presentations to learn how we’re transforming supply chains in the energy sector. Whether you’re planning an S/4HANA migration or seeking ways to optimize fuel retail operations, Implico is here to help. 

 

Our Product Portfolio: Driving Efficiency Across the Supply Chain 

Implico’s comprehensive portfolio empowers businesses to streamline processes, reduce costs, and improve customer satisfaction.  

Learn more about our core offerings: 

  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** (Secondary Distribution Management) - Optimizing last-mile fuel delivery through route planning, dispatching, and real-time monitoring. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** (Retail Fuel Network Operations) - Enhancing visibility into fuel station inventory, pricing, and replenishment needs. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** - Automating route planning and dispatch to reduce downtime and maximize resource efficiency. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION ***- Simplifying and automating processes in bulk liquid handling, from loading to transport. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION ***- Streamlining cylinder management, automating order processing, and optimizing distribution for maximum efficiency. 

 

Let’s shape the future of energy together! 

Schedule a meeting with our team:  

Visit *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** to contact us or book a session during the event. 

 

We look forward to seeing you in Rotterdam from April 8-10, 2025!

entry.title: Unlocking Data Accessibility: How Accenture Leverages Generative AI RAG for Finance Teams
entry.content:
The Challenge

Finance teams need quick access to crucial information on best practices, accounting guidelines, compliance procedures, and policy requirements. However, this information is often scattered across various platforms and formats, making retrieval difficult, especially under time constraints.

The challenge of navigating this complex landscape can lead to slower decision-making and inconsistencies in global policy application. As teams grow and technologies evolve, ensuring that everyone has the latest material can become a burden on experienced team members.

A streamlined, accessible, and consistently updated knowledge base is needed to empower finance team members. A solution that consolidates information and enhances accessibility through intuitive, everyday language searches would alleviate information overload and enable efficient, data-driven decision-making across Accenture's global finance operations.

 

The Solution

Accenture has implemented an innovative pilot solution leveraging Generative AI, specifically Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology. This advanced approach integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with information retrieval systems, enabling finance teams to access and interact with vast amounts of data using natural, everyday business language.

At the core of this solution is the utilization of vector embeddings and similarity search within a vector database. By employing chunking strategies, large documents and datasets are broken down into manageable pieces, or "chunks." Each chunk is then converted into a high-dimensional vector using embedding functions, which preserve the semantic similarity of the content. These vectors are stored and managed within the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, which supports create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations using SQL.

When a user inputs a query, the system converts this query into a vector representation. Through similarity search, the system compares this query vector against the stored vectors to find the most relevant chunks of information. This process enables the retrieval of highly pertinent data from across the knowledge base, which the LLM then incorporates to generate accurate and contextually enriched responses.

Key Features of the Solution:

  • Semantic Search and Similarity Matching: By understanding the semantic meaning behind queries and documents, the system can retrieve information that is contextually relevant, even if the exact keywords do not match.
  • Natural Language Processing (NLP): Users can interact with the system using natural language, making the search process intuitive and user-friendly.
  • Consistency and Up-to-Date Guidance: The solution taps into an agreed-upon "best practice knowledge and rules database," ensuring that the guidance provided is consistent and reflects the most current policies and procedures.
  • Empowering New-Joiners & Experts Alike: By providing quick and accurate answers, the tool empowers users to be more self-sufficient, reducing the need for constant support from seasoned Finance professionals.
  •  Enhanced Onboarding Experience: New team members access information seamlessly, facilitating quicker integration and contribution to finance operations.

To consolidate disparate information sources and enhance accessibility through intuitive search capabilities using everyday business language, Accenture explored and piloted an innovative solution utilizing SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Database (Vector DB) offering.

 

 



Exploring the SAP HANA Vector DB Offering

At the heart of Accenture's solution lies the integration of Generative AI with advanced data retrieval systems. Specifically, the team leveraged Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology, which combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with information retrieval mechanisms. This integration enables finance teams to interact with vast amounts of data using natural language, making the search process more intuitive and efficient.

The SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB plays a crucial role in this setup. By utilizing vector embeddings and similarity search capabilities, the system transforms large documents and datasets into high-dimensional vectors that capture the semantic essence of the content. This process involves:

  1. Chunking: Breaking down large documents into manageable pieces or "chunks" to facilitate efficient processing. In our case the chunk size of 1000 words was found to be effective. You may need to experiment a bit to find the size that works for you.
  2. Embedding: Useinf the ADA models from openai we converted each chunk into a numerical vector representation using embedding models that understand semantic relationships.
  3. Vector Storage: SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB was leveraged, which supports comprehensive create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations through SQL
  4. Retrieval: This is where similarity search plays an important role, we needed to use a reranking step before the final results were generated for the user.

Key Technical Considerations

When designing this solution, these were a few critical factors to work through:

  • Security: It was an important requirement to maintain security between the documents of different teams which is established by ensuring different vector tables for different teams.
  • Vector DB – Auto Updates: Automated chunking process and update of Vector DB tables based on user uploaded files on SharePoint. No IT administration is needed, and the business team is directly responsible for maintaining the document store through this process.
  • Authorization: The system validates if a user belongs to a specific team and ensures only the vector tables which contains documents for that specific team is used for retrieval

Responsible AI, Data Privacy and Security are at the core of how Accenture designs Generative AI solutions. Ensuring that sensitive financial data remains secure within the vector embeddings that are required robust access control mechanisms.


Conclusion

Accenture's innovative Generative AI solution with Retrieval-Augmented Generation revolutionizes financial data access by enabling intuitive and efficient natural language searches. By integrating SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Database, which uses vector embeddings and similarity search, the system ensures secure, team-specific document retrieval.

This approach not only enhances internal processes but also reduces reliance on specialized support, promoting efficiency and consistency across global finance operations. With a focus on responsible AI, Accenture ensures robust data privacy and security, making their solution both powerful and trustworthy.

entry.title: HANA Cloud Data Lake - SQL on Files (CSV, PARQUET) changes in QRC 4/2024
entry.content:
As per SAP Note - 3508244 in QRC 4/2024, a change is introduced in SQL for Files. The Data Lake Relational Engine native SQL on Files has been deprecated from the beginning of QRC 4/2024. Post this update, database users must access the Data Lake Files container using SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database.

This below provides details on the change and guides you on the process of using the SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database native SQL on Files instead of the Data Lake Relational Engine native SQL on Files. 

The SAP HANA native SQL on Files feature allows SAP HANA Database users to perform query processing on open-format files (CSV, PARQUET) stored in the Data Lake Files. There is no need to import files into the SAP HANA Database, and there is no requirement to configure or use the Data Lake Relational Engine anymore. The files can be retained in existing Data Lake Files container, but starting from QRC 4/2024, queries must be executed from SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database, rather than from the Data Lake Relational Engine. These queries will use SAP HANA native SQL, replacing the use of Data Lake Relational Engine native SQL. 

Below are the steps that needs to be performed for using SAP HANA native SQL on Files:

Step 1: Login in to the Database Explorer of SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database and open SQL Console. Make sure the data source file is present in the SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Filesystem

Step 2: Create PSE. Use the following SQL query to create a PSE named 'SOF_TEST'.

 

CREATE PSE SOF_TEST;

 

Please note that SOF_TEST Is an example name. Replace it with the required source name.

Step 3: Add the SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Filesystem Certificate Chain using the below query.

 

ALTER PSE SOF_TEST SET OWN CERTIFICATE 
'-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
Clientkey
-----END PRIVATE KEY-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MainCertificate
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Intermediatecertificate
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
RootCertificate
-----END CERTIFICATE-----';

 

Please provide the entire content of the certificate and key between -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- and -----END CERTIFICATE----- & -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----- and -----END PRIVATE KEY-----.

Step 4: Create Remote Source. Run the following SQL query to create the remote source in HANA Cloud DB for the HANA Data Lake.

 

CREATE REMOTE SOURCE REMOTE_SOF ADAPTER "file" CONFIGURATION '
provider=hdlf;
endpoint=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.files.hdl.prod-eu10.hanacloud.ondemand.com;'
WITH CREDENTIAL TYPE 'X509' PSE SOF_TEST;

 

Kindly change the SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake REST API endpoint URL as per the requirement

Step 5: Create Virtual Table. After the remote source is set up, create a virtual table in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database that points to the path and file present in the remote source.

  • If a CSV file is present in SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Filesystem root path (/), run the below query. 

 

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE VT_EMPLOYEE (EMPLOYEEID INT PRIMARY KEY, FIRSTNAME VARCHAR(50), LASTNAME VARCHAR(50)) AT REMOTE_SOF."/EMPLOYEE.csv" AS CSV FIELD DELIMITED BY ',';

 

  •  If a PARQUET file is present in SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Filesystem root path (/), run the below query. 

 

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE VT_EMPLOYEE (EMPLOYEEID INT PRIMARY KEY, FIRSTNAME VARCHAR(50), LASTNAME VARCHAR(50)) AT REMOTE_SOF."/EMPLOYEE.parquet" AS PARQUET;

 

Kindly note the following:

  • Table name VT_EMPLOYEE & columns EMPLOYEEID, FIRSTNAME and LASTNAME are examples. Replace it with required table name and columns.
  • Path and Filename /EMPLOYEE.csv and /EMPLOYEE.parquet are examples. Replace it complete SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake path and valid filename. 

Step 6: Query the virtual table from SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL Console 

 

SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEE;

 

The data present in the SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake (CSV and PARQUET) files will be displayed as the content of the virtual table VT_EMPLOYEE.
